feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Identity Services
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Identity Services Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Handle persons leaving the organisation when provisioning with IPS
entry.content:
Dear all,

 

We are setting up provisioning from EntraID to IAS to S/4 onprem. 

When a person leaves the organisation, the useraccount might get deleted in EntraID. From audit perspective we do not want to delete the user in S/4 but lock it and assign a specific usergroup in SU01. 
I am trying to find out what the best way is to achieve this. 

I found this parameter ias.user.update.instead.delete which might be of help, but I only found a SAP help page on it and nothing else on Google, so my guess is, nobody is using it. 

 

How did other setup the handling of preventing users from being deleted in an S/4 backend when persons leave the organisation irt IPS provisioning?

Thanks for your time

Sander

SAP Cloud Identity Services SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: How to block anyone from accessing the BTP outside a particular geo-location
entry.content:
Our usecase:
Allow the use of BTP(business as well as platform users) only within a specified IP range(Geo-location). 
Challenge
When we try to use RBA(risk based authentication) in the Cloud Identity Services, we need to create a rule for each IP range(CIDR). This is not practical as we have nearly a million records and creating a rule for each record is impractical. Also there is no excel upload option.

entry.title: User deactivate via IPS
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We provision users from Azure -> IAS -> Enable Now using IPS.

If a user for example:  test1) is removed/deleted from the group in Azure/ IAS, and an IPS Job runs afterward, the user (test1) is not deleted in Enable Now; only their roles are removed. However, the user remains "Active" in Enable Now.

Can the user in Enable Now be deactivated (i.e., the "Active" checkbox removed) through the IPS Job if the user is deleted/removed from the Azure/IAS group?

Or is there a way to automatically deactivate the user in Enable Now instead of directly deleting them?

 Does anyone have an idea ?

Many Thanks

Best Regards

entry.title: Significance of Origin Key in BTP Trust, especially sap.custom
entry.content:
Hi all,

we are about to onboard SAP Digital Manufacturing. With this comes BTP and establishing trust between subaccount and Cloud Integration Services.

That being said, when using the "Establish Trust" wizard, the default value for "Origin Key" will be "sap.custom".

I know about the overall posibility to use multiple Origin Key values to separate different IdPs / trusts within one subaccount, something that is feasible but not the norm, see Using Multiple Identity Providers from the Same Subaccount, however, I'm unsure about the significance of the wizard's default value "sap.custom"

My questions

  1. Is there some kind of built in "fallback" or hard wired lookup for "sap.custom"? In other words, would it be a bad idea to not have at least one entry with "sap.custom" (providing we are going the route of using Cloud Identity Services and not the default IdP)
  2. For our use case we have SAP Digital Manufacturing deployed in the subaccount and nothing more. Am I right that in this case it might be just fine to stay with the "sap.custom" moniker or is it (when planning ahead) a good idea to apply a more meaningful naming convention? And if so, what should I name the darn thing then? cis_dev (for cloud identity services development environment / tier) or something else?

Many thanks and cheers

J

entry.title: User not updated by SAP IPS Sync job from S4 Cloud to IAS
entry.content:
Dear All,

Post ReSync and Read job from S4 cloud to IAS via IPS sync job, the user's validity date in S4 did not get updated in  IAS. Please suggest if and how validity and other user attributes from S4 to IAS

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: SAP IAS - Do we need users in SAP IAS if we use corporate IDP for Authentication
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

If we have corporate IDP and IAS is used as proxy IDP, do we need to sync the users from any source systems to SAP IAS?  I know for the fact that for SF story reporting the users must exist in SAP IAS. But apart from that only for authentication to any other application, do we need users in SAP IAS when we use corporate IDP ?

Here is a use case: 

I want all the users to get authenticated against the corporate IDP (e.g. Azure), when they are accessing any of the below applications. So in that case do we need the corporate users to exist in IAS? 

1. S4 HANA Public Cloud

2. SAP SAC

3.SAP ARIBA

Thanks

Sanjay

entry.title: How to use Extensions with IPS and User attributes
entry.content:
Hello,

 

In the IAS you have users. There is a tab containing extensions. I found out that custom schemas can be assigned to users. You can manually assign a custom schema to a user. But I want to automate this. 

How can I achieve this?

Source is EntraID. Target is IAS.

created a custom schema urn:mycomp:Apps. Created three names, sapdev, sapqas and sapprod.

When a new user is read from EntraID the custom attribute sapdev should be set to true.

In IAS I want then to be able to see that attribute set to True,

And in the next provisioning run, I want to be able to select my users based on that attribute. 

 

Is this possible?

 

Thanks

Sander

 

SAP Cloud Identity Services

entry.title: Hardcoding users in transformation logic of IPS to provide access for SAC Reporting
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We need to hardcode users in IPS to grant access to Story Reports. Earlier, we were using the sf.user.filter in the properties and added the respective usernames there. But there are more than 100 users now to be hardcoded and we cannot continue to add in this filter because of the character limit.

Can anybody give an idea if this hardcoding for providing access to SAC reporting can be done via transformation logic of source/target system?

What code can be added to hardcode such users?

Thanks in advance for your response.

entry.title: SAP IAS and Successfactors - How SAML2 Attributes are used
entry.content:
Hi

I created this post for better understanding how it works. It isn't a post how to configure/fix errors. Just for better understanding of process. Our configuration works, but in some special cases I would like to extend my knowledge about.

We have a situation
SF is integrated with IAS and IAS is federated with CorporateIDP EntraID (no enriched an so one) 

On Entra ID side we have a configuration 

 

UPN has format of the email address and is used as the login in EntraID
but some partners have own Email outside our domain as on example below

We have a SF app - standard configuration delivered by SAP 

with standard attributes 

App is federated with CorporateIDP

User in SF and IAS had following settings

Same login name and mail as on SF side 

But user wasn't able to logon to SF (IAS/EntraID logon was ok)

In IAS log we had 

Of course, after changing email on IAS side from first.last@domain.pl to username@partner.com logon to SF is possible,


Looking in the documentation (maybe I do not understand whole process correctly) 
 

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/corp-idp-configure-identity-federation?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/configure-identity-federation-for-applications

When Use Identity Authentication user store option is enabled, the application checks if the users authenticated by the corporate identity provider exist in the Identity Directory, the user store of Identity Authentication. The existence check is done with the name identifier sent by the corporate identity provider for the identifying attributes uid, loginName, emailsand phoneNumber.

For users that exist in the Identity Directory, data from the user store is taken and the subject name identifier, assertion and default attributes according to the application configuration are sent. For users with no profile in Identity Authentication, the application receives the nameID attribute from the corporate IdP assertion, and the attributes according to the application configuration.

 

In my understanding, if federation is enabled, attributes should be taken from user record in IAS. But by some reasons mail from CorporateIDP is used.
Question why? 

Thanks for any suggestions & feedback 🙂
Artur

entry.title: Provision User via IPS to SAP Concur via Identity v4 API
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I hope my question finds you well!

Does anyone have experience with provisioning users from SAP Cloud Identity Services – Identity Directory via SAP IPS using the latest API version, Identity v4 API (also known as IPS property concur.api.version = 2)?

We were able to provision users with their core identity information (part of the main identity), but are not able to maintain attributes that are part of Spend or Travel. Based on what we found in this documentation:
https://developer.concur.com/api-reference/user-provisioning/v4.user-provisioning.html

…it seems that you need to call the UPS (User Provisioning Service) in order to maintain all attributes. This URL should follow the pattern:

https://{datacenterURI}/profile/v4/Users/

What SAP IPS does instead is call the following URL:

https://{datacenterURI}/profile/identity/v4/Users/

Does anyone know if there is a way to determine which URL is used by SAP IPS? It seems that the property concur.api.version defines which API URL is being used.

 

EDIT:

As described here, it seems that SAP IPS is restricted from maintaining the Spend Profile.

However, we think that it might be possible to achieve this using the IPS target system type "SCIM-System" to reach the following API URL:

https://{datacenterURI}/profile/v4/Users/

Any feedback from someone who has tried this before or has any further information about integrating SAP IPS would be much appreciated 🙂

Thank you in advance for your feedback and kind regards,
Alex

entry.title: Access to Workforce Time via IAS
entry.content:
Hello!

We are looking for ways to link Workforce Software with SAP's Identity Authentication Service (IAS).
Does anyone know of any articles or have they done this internally?

Best regards,
Rhuan Castro.

#IAS #WorkforceTime

entry.title: How to populate validTo in IAS from SuccessFactors via IPS
entry.content:
Hi there,

We use SuccessFactors with IAS/IPS.  We have two transformation scripts in IPS with our mappings for Source And Target systems.  Up til now this has been fine and we've added in numerous fields, but I need to add in CompanyExitDate from Successfactors into IAS field validTo. It's a standard User field, so I thought this would be straightforward  - mistake...

In Source Transformation I have: 

{
"sourcePath": "$.companyExitDate",
"optional": true,
"targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User']['validTo']"
},

and in Target transformation I have 

{
"sourcePath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User']['validTo']",
"optional": true,
"targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User']['validTo']"
},

But nothing populates for people with a CompanyExitDate. 

Any ideas what I'm doing wrong?

Thanks in Advance!

entry.title: Admin. users of IAS through IAG or GRC access request
entry.content:
Hi All,

can Administrator users of type 'User' and 'System' be created from GRC(using GRC-IAG bridge) or through IAG access requests

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: BTP Trial IAS Provisioning Issue
entry.content: “My BTP trial global account ID is 58dc44e9trial in us10. Identity Authentication token URL exists, but the admin UI and SAML metadata both return errors (Uh oh, 404). I'm unable to establish trust to subscribe to SAP Build Work Zone. Please advise.”

entry.title: IAS Certificate: Configuring IAS SAML 2.0 Integration in the Admin Center
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team, today a certificate will expire in IAS. We have already followed the instructions and downloaded the metadata from SF and uploaded it to the right place in IAS. Unfortunately, the expiration date of the certificate has not yet changed. I therefore urgently request your support or feedback on what to do here.
Many thanks and best regards

entry.title: SAP CIS: SAP BTP/Fiori login as other user (proxy)
entry.content:
Dear community,

customer had SAP SuccessFactors as an Identity Provider for SAP BTP with SAP Fiori apps.

There were able to login to SAP SF as admin, proxy to another user and then go to SAP Fiori as that proxied user.

After the transition to SAP CIS both SAP SF and SAP BTP / Fiori are applications in SAP CIS.

The proxy functionality as known before doesn't work - CIS is sending to SAP BTP, during login, admin account instead of user account.

1) Is proxy possible in that architecture?

2) Do You have any other ideas how to login to SAP BTP / Fiori as other user without knowing/changing it's password?

3) I've tried to change login name in SAP CIS from admin to user, but that also doesn't work properly - maybe because in BTP users are created as "shadow users"?

Thank You for all the help You can give me!

Best Regards,

Saturnin Swędrak

entry.title: sf.group.filter in IPS
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to filter a specific permission group in IPS sf.group.filter. I am using API Version 2. It seems that this logic:

displayName eq "xy"

does not work? What is the right Value to filter a specific Permission Group?

 

Thanks

entry.title: Transformation Mapping IPS Groups to SAP Datasphere Profiles
entry.content:
Hello,

I’m currently working on setting up a transformation mapping between an IPS group and SAP Datasphere.
Here is the transformation code I’m using:

{
                "condition": "$.groups[?(@.display == 'GLB APP SAP IAS_DEV BTP Datasphere DEV TESTSCIM')] != []",
                "constant": {
                    "value": "PROFILE:t.MGQALN:EK_SR_P_WORKSHOP",
                    "display": "EK_SR_P_WORKSHOP",
                    "primary": true
                },
                "targetPath": "$.roles[0]"
            },
            {
                "condition": "$.groups[?(@.display == 'GLB APP SAP IAS_DEV BTP Datasphere DEV TESTSCIM_ADMIN')] != []",
                "constant": {
                    "value": "PROFILE:sap.dwc:Data_Warehouse_Cloud_Administrator",
                    "display": "Data_Warehouse_Cloud_Administrator",
                    "primary": true
                },
                "targetPath": "$.roles[1]"
            }

 

What works with this:

If the user is assigned to one or both groups, they are successfully created in SAP Datasphere.
The corresponding profile (role) is also assigned.

What doesn’t work:

If I remove a group, the associated profile in SAP Datasphere is not removed.



Does anyone have an idea how to solve this?

Best regards,
Andreas

entry.title: IAS ERROR READ & REASYNC JOB
entry.content:
Hello! Thank you in advance to everyone who took the time to respond to this post.

We are currently receiving an error when reading and resynchronizing users in IAS.

The source system is in SAP SuccessFactors, and the destination system is IAS and SAC.

The error we received doesn't give us much information. However, when running the JOB, we see that a connection is made to the source system, but it fails to communicate with the destination system.

I'm sharing the error:

user:66717a4d-6cf8-44cb-b913-d10a4f83bbba,

system=Identity Authentication - a1e71dzuj - target,

time=2025-06-06T22:02:12.329+00,

error=HTTP operation failed invoking https://a1e71dzuj.accounts.ondemand.com/service/scim/Users with statusCode: 401, Response: ,
 
content={"active":true,"applicationId":"null","displayName":"TESTUSER ADMIN","emails":[{"value":"sfadmin@TESTUSER.com.mx"}],"mailVerified":"true","name":{"familyName":"ADMIN","givenName":"TESTUSER"},"passwordStatus":"disabled","schemas":["urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:User","urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User","urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:custom:2.0:User"],"sendMail":"false","urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:2.0:User":{"division":"N/A","employeeNumber":"111117"},"urn:sap:cloud:scim:schemas:extension:custom:2.0:User":{"attributes":[{"name":"customAttribute1","value":"149774B2A4824FF68C0E9763FB068798"}]},"userName":"111117","userType":"EMPLOYEE"}
 
Interestingly, the same configuration of both the target system and the source system (transformations and properties) are the same and in QAS it works correctly.
 

I appreciate your comments.

If you need any visual help with either the transformation or the properties, please let me know so I can share them.

Thanks again!

entry.title: IAS/IPS – Efficient Group Setup?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We are using SAP IAS/IPS with Azure as Idp. We have already connected several SAP Cloud Apps to IAS, and more apps will be added.

Access to these apps is controlled by groups in Azure. For example, depending on a user’s role in SAC or Enable Now, different groups are created in Azure.

In SAC, we already have at least 10 groups, and in Enable Now even more. As more SAP Cloud Apps are connected, the number of Azure groups increases quickly.

My question is:
Is there a better or easier way to manage roles and access without creating so many groups in Azure?

How do you manage this in your environment to keep it organized and scalable?

Thanks for your help

Best regards
